body.title: Canonical machine name registry with explicit aliases
body.result: 1947e3c9 — Igor's unfiled draft from 2026-04-12 finally lands. Discovery: registry infrastructure (machines.json with aliases, machine_manager.resolve_alias) already existed. Missing piece was a tool surface. Added machine_lookup(name) and machine_list_all() tools. Plus get_all_machines() helper in machine_manager that returns offline/unranked machines too (pi/asus) — distinct from get_ranked_machines which filters for inference routing. Tools never pattern-match; unregistered names get [NOT REGISTERED] with clear guidance. 12 new tests including the exact 'yoga9i' case Igor flagged. 2067 total pass, zero regressions. Honors Igor's acceptance criteria.
body.description:
Igor drafted this ticket on 2026-04-12 but never filed it (reply-obligation-fork bug). From transcript /home/akien/.TheIgors/akien/outbox/20260412.IkienIgorChatWThreadProblems.txt:

Problem: Informal machine name abbreviations (e.g. 'yoga9i' for 'akienyoga9i', 'yoga' for 'yoga9i') are being generated ad-hoc by Igor and CC, creating drift risk as the cluster grows. CC at one point thought 'yoga9i' WAS the host's name and kept saying it wasn't up.

Goal: Canonical machine name registry mapping full hostname → registered short aliases → role/IP. All cluster tooling resolves names through the registry. Unregistered short names rejected, not pattern-matched.

Proposed scheme: yoga9i stays (already short and common), dell for akiendelllinux. No further shortening without registration.

Acceptance: Registry file exists. At least one tool (Igor's resolver, CC's machine references) uses it. Igor's procedural memory updated so he stops generating drift aliases.

Risk: Low. Mostly documentation + registry file.
